essence: David aligns the team on three-tier data environments (dev/staging/production — 'staging' deliberately chosen over UAT so AI agents parse it correctly), a non-traditional visual-first build order enabled by the CLI tool, and the handover-document pattern that in two weeks turned Angela into an effective domain-expert prompt engineer he can offload to.

Decision to get client feedback on participant onboarding early rather than waiting for polish — the flow is demoable this week/next with a rough interface, improved later, and which client sees what depends on the conversation.

actions.text: Start showing the participant onboarding flow to a client this week or next with a rough interface

loop.label: early client feedback on participant onboarding

essence: David reiterates the CLI is an undervalued core MVP capability (data into the system before client-facing screens exist) and that Mochaccino can turn client feedback plus the schema into a working visualization in about an hour — instantly revealing whether a request is achievable or exposes a schema gap.

David explains the MVP's primary purpose is defining and validating the data schema (80-85% of its code carries to production) while doubling as a CLI remote-control for the main app; the Lego escape-room analogy separates schema from UI, and Mochaccino does schema-constrained mockups with gap analysis back to David when Angela's desired UX can't be built.

David defines requirements for his own observability/session-analysis app (AngelEye genesis): monitor chats and tool activity for recurring phrases to suggest skills, expose BMAD story context as generated images, scoped filtering by tool/event/hook/agent/project, and session naming/tagging for long-running memory — the Dizzler mixing-board UI judged too noisy and global.

actions.text: Stop the Dizzler application and review it without letting it influence the new build
Get front-end designer observations on Dizzler via background tasks
Propose 10 names for the new observability app using the stack naming conventions document

essence: Idea to extend the toolchain skill so it can run automation itself (Ansible playbooks, root -v) with built-in daily/weekly caching instead of refreshing every use; David doubts Claude replay adds value since its underlying data may already be accessible, and asks for the Dizzler agent config to be completed and restarted.

actions.text: Complete Dizzler agent configuration, restart it, and confirm whether Claude sessions need restarting

essence: David questions why every tool (RayPlay VCR, appydave machine hostname) invents a new configuration file location — new Claude installs see no data until reconfigured — and asks what past projects did, wanting a consistent centralized machine-config approach instead of scattered files.

David proposes a 'What do I have' system-capability skill as a sibling to Who Am I, asks how to get Dizzler webhooks and Claude Replay hooks working for multi-agent observability, and demands richer test reporting than bare pass/fail plus UAT checks tied to incidents (at minimum an incident list).

actions.text: Run a background task to plan the 'What do I have' system-state skill
Set up webhooks for Dizzler multi-agent observability and any Claude Replay hooks
Verify an incident list exists and UAT coverage for incidents

essence: Debating whether the UAT list should be entity-centric, workflow-centric, or hybrid so Playwright MCP runs can execute overnight without burning tokens or wrecking the database; clarified 'Angela feedback' (domain expert/BA) is not UAT; flagged that the app always starting logged-out slows everything and a saved-session resume may fix it.

actions.text: Investigate saved-session mechanism so the app resumes logged-in instead of starting from the not-logged-in state
Improve plugin reload output to surface context about the last work done

essence: David splits Nano Banana into two build tracks — image generation (Jan's side) and a context-shaping skill that turns video/BMAD session data into JSON — exchanged through FliHub via git with regular commits and bidirectional pull/push like Signal Studio's notification pattern; meanwhile he demands the UAT-vs-E2E naming clusterfuck be unravelled (UAT = human-run acceptance tests, never E2E) after days lost to rabbit holes.

actions.text: Unravel the UAT vs E2E naming conflict and align tests with the system design
Start building the Nano Banana skill from a handover document into appydave-plugins
Deep dive FliHub assets and inboxes concepts and design git-based bidirectional image workflow with Jan
Run A/B research tests on the cloned memory systems and report build-vs-adopt findings

essence: Nano Banana must run outside any single session and pull data from multiple JSON documents/sessions with easy DX (no manual session-ID copying); David asks Jan for any working image-gen skills (Nano Banana 2/Pro, NotebookLM) to fold into his research, and briefs him on the Coding Lab Claude livestreams and Tuesday 4pm Nimman Chiang Mai meetup ahead of designing an event-page visual.

actions.text: Jan to share any working image-generation skill progress and help design the Claude event page visual
actions.target: Chiang Mai AI Community event page

essence: David wants near-real-time Nano Banana image support while recording BMAD v6 SupportSignal videos — scanning live session data for patterns mid-terminal, aggregating all sessions into a central store (leaning Supabase over local SQL) with a hybrid real-time/batch approach — while flagging multi-terminal race conditions and venting that the CLAUDE.md template lags new Claude commands (loop, by-the-way, summary).

actions.text: Update CLAUDE.md and templates to document new Claude commands (loop, by-the-way, summary) and surface them proactively
Fix the page UI so multiple pages can be added, deleted, and clicked

essence: David sets the rule that server data lives at the monorepo root (never in source dirs) for AppyStack/DeckHand, asks how Bitfocus architecture affects the Ecamm skill and DeckHand, and commissions a fact sheet plus open-source tool research for capturing the full Claude Code stream (prompts, responses, tools, subagents, swarms, hooks) — noting rich JSONL transcripts alone might be enough.

actions.text: Move server data storage to the monorepo root with docs, unit tests, and a handover note for DeckHand
Research top open-source observability contenders (stars, commit activity) adaptable to Claude Code
Run Playwright MCP smoke test on DeckHand and verify deckhand.json has not regressed

essence: David coaches Brandy on turning 20 photos into a slideshow video (numbered folder then FFmpeg or a drag-and-drop tool via a Claude prompt) and shares his suggestive-over-prescriptive prompting philosophy — give the AI room to be the boss.

David reverse-engineered Ecamm Live's undocumented server and plist files for scene IDs and crop values but hit hard limits — no programmatic crop panning (scene file only saves on app close) and the HTTP control port changes every launch forcing slow port scans; Stream Deck's SDK is closed so he rebuilt controls in his own app from screenshots; a powered USB hub should fix wake-from-sleep dropouts and Bitfocus Companion is the next integration path.

actions.text: Have Claude go deeper on Bitfocus Companion as an Ecamm integration path
Get a powered USB hub to fix Stream Deck wake-from-sleep dropouts
